auto_merge.commit_message:
Cython 3.3.0a1 introduced a regression where C++ comparisons (e.g.
`unique_ptr != NULL`) generate ambiguous C++ code that fails to compile
with GCC 14.

Fixes https://github.com/cython/cython/issues/7765

## Root Cause

Commit 23581d608 ("Always evaluate comparisons as bint if that is the
expected result") changed `coerce_to_boolean` to unconditionally set
`is_temp = True`. This routes C++ comparison codegen through
`generate_operation_code()`, which casts both operands to a
`common_type` via `result_as()`. For C++ types like `unique_ptr`, this generates:

```cpp
(__pyx_v_w->c_obj != ((std::unique_ptr<MyStruct> )NULL));
```

GCC 14 reports this as ambiguous because `NULL` (`__null`) matches both
the `pointer` and `nullptr_t` constructors of `unique_ptr`.

## Fix

Since `analyse_cpp_comparison()` already coerces operands to the
resolved operator's argument types, the subsequent
`result_as(common_type)` cast is redundant for C++ comparisons. This PR
skips it and uses the operands' `result()` directly when
`is_cpp_comparison()` is true.

## Test

Added tests covering:
- `unique_ptr == NULL` / `!= NULL`
- `unique_ptr == nullptr` / `!= nullptr`
- `unique_ptr` in boolean context (`if ptr:`)
auto_merge.commit_title: Fix ambiguous unique_ptr(NULL) comparison in C++ mode (GH-7766)

repo.description: The most widely used Python to C compiler
repo.name: cython

body:
## Summary

AI disclosure: I provided an example of some code that was previously working under older Cython versions that was failing under Cython 3.3.0a1 to an agent, and it wrote up issue #7765 and came up with the fix in this PR. I verified that its tests are a faithful representation of the issue I observed, and the fix looks reasonable to me, but there may be a more concise fix here.

Fixes #7765.

Cython 3.3.0a1 introduced a regression where C++ comparisons (e.g. `unique_ptr != NULL`) generate ambiguous C++ code that fails to compile with GCC 14.

## Root Cause

Commit 23581d608 ("Always evaluate comparisons as bint if that is the expected result") changed `coerce_to_boolean` to unconditionally set `is_temp = True`. This routes C++ comparison codegen through `generate_operation_code()`, which casts both operands to a `common_type` via `result_as()`. For C++ types like `unique_ptr`, this generates:

```cpp
(__pyx_v_w->c_obj != ((std::unique_ptr<MyStruct> )NULL));
```

GCC 14 reports this as ambiguous because `NULL` (`__null`) matches both the `pointer` and `nullptr_t` constructors of `unique_ptr`.

## Fix

Since `analyse_cpp_comparison()` already coerces operands to the resolved operator's argument types, the subsequent `result_as(common_type)` cast is redundant for C++ comparisons. This PR skips it and uses the operands' `result()` directly when `is_cpp_comparison()` is true.

## Test

Added `tests/run/cpp_unique_ptr_null_comparison.pyx` covering:
- `unique_ptr == NULL` / `!= NULL`
- `unique_ptr == nullptr` / `!= nullptr`
- `unique_ptr` in boolean context (`if ptr:`)

comment_data.body: Unless there's a good reason not to, I think this should be moved to `tests/run/cpp_smart_ptr.pyx` (just because adding a new test file costs notably more CI time than expanding an existing file).

The basic fix looks reasonable to me.

I wonder if it might be cleaner to move `if self.is_cpp_comparsion()` up here to a separate block (along with the `translate_cpp_exception` block below).

Just because the `is_cpp_comparison` block doesn't really look to share that much with rest of the code here.

`code1` and `code2` aren't overly helpful names (which you just inherited here), and their expression is short enough now to go into an f-string.
```suggestion
            statement = f"{result_code} = {coerce_result}({operand1.result()} {self.c_operator(op)} {operand2.result()});"
```
comment_data.diff_hunk:
@@ -14270,6 +14270,30 @@ def generate_operation_code(self, code, result_code,
                 operand1.result(),
                 operand2.result()))
 
+        elif self.is_cpp_comparison():
+            # C++ comparisons already had operands coerced in
+            # analyse_cpp_comparison(). Don't re-cast them to a
+            # common_type, as casting NULL to a C++ class type
+            # (e.g. unique_ptr) generates ambiguous code with GCC 14.
+            code1 = operand1.result()
+            code2 = operand2.result()
+            statement = "%s = %s(%s %s %s);" % (
+                result_code,
+                coerce_result,
+                code1,
+                self.c_operator(op),
+                code2)

comment_data.body:
And since you're already touching this line, let's just update it as well.
```suggestion
            code.putln(f"{result_code} = {coerce_result}({code1} {self.c_operator(op)} {code2});")
```
comment_data.diff_hunk:
@@ -14282,22 +14306,12 @@ def generate_operation_code(self, code, result_code,
                 common_type = type1
             code1 = operand1.result_as(common_type)
             code2 = operand2.result_as(common_type)
-            statement = "%s = %s(%s %s %s);" % (
+            code.putln("%s = %s(%s %s %s);" % (
                 result_code,
                 coerce_result,
                 code1,
                 self.c_operator(op),
-                code2)
-            if self.is_cpp_comparison() and self.exception_check == '+':
-                translate_cpp_exception(
-                    code,
-                    self.pos,
-                    statement,
-                    result_code if self.type.is_pyobject else None,
-                    self.exception_value,
-                    self.in_nogil_context)
-            else:
-                code.putln(statement)
+                code2))

comment_data.body: Pointer comparisons _can_ use `==` and `!=`, but I would generally prefer `is` and `is not`. We should test both variants here.
comment_data.diff_hunk:
@@ -114,3 +114,66 @@ def test_dynamic_pointer_cast():
     a = shared_ptr[A](new A())
     b = dynamic_pointer_cast[B, A](a)
     assert b.get() == NULL
+
+
+cdef extern from *:
+    """
+    struct MyStruct {
+        int value;
+    };
+    """
+    cdef cppclass MyStruct:
+        int value
+
+
+cdef class Wrapper:
+    cdef unique_ptr[MyStruct] c_obj
+
+    def set_obj(self):
+        self.c_obj.reset(new MyStruct())
+
+
+def test_unique_ptr_null_comparison():
+    """
+    >>> test_unique_ptr_null_comparison()
+    True
+    False
+    True
+    """
+    cdef Wrapper w = Wrapper()
+    print(w.c_obj == NULL)
+    print(w.c_obj != NULL)

comment_data.body: I'm not convinced `is` and `is not` should work here, since these comparisons are using an overloaded c++ `operator==`
comment_data.diff_hunk:
@@ -114,3 +114,66 @@ def test_dynamic_pointer_cast():
     a = shared_ptr[A](new A())
     b = dynamic_pointer_cast[B, A](a)
     assert b.get() == NULL
+
+
+cdef extern from *:
+    """
+    struct MyStruct {
+        int value;
+    };
+    """
+    cdef cppclass MyStruct:
+        int value
+
+
+cdef class Wrapper:
+    cdef unique_ptr[MyStruct] c_obj
+
+    def set_obj(self):
+        self.c_obj.reset(new MyStruct())
+
+
+def test_unique_ptr_null_comparison():
+    """
+    >>> test_unique_ptr_null_comparison()
+    True
+    False
+    True
+    """
+    cdef Wrapper w = Wrapper()
+    print(w.c_obj == NULL)
+    print(w.c_obj != NULL)

commit.message:
Fix ambiguous unique_ptr(NULL) comparison in C++ mode

When a C++ comparison (e.g. unique_ptr != NULL) was used in a boolean
context, the refactored coerce_to_boolean (commit 23581d608) sets
is_temp=True, routing codegen through generate_operation_code() which
casts both operands to a common_type via result_as(). For C++ types
like unique_ptr, this generates ((std::unique_ptr<T>)NULL) which is
ambiguous with GCC 14 because NULL matches both the pointer and
nullptr_t constructors.

Since analyse_cpp_comparison() already coerces operands to the resolved
operator's argument types, the subsequent result_as(common_type) cast
is redundant for C++ comparisons. Skip it and use the operands'
result() directly.

Closes #7765

Fix ambiguous unique_ptr(NULL) comparison in C++ mode

When a C++ comparison (e.g. unique_ptr != NULL) was used in a boolean
context, the refactored coerce_to_boolean (commit 23581d608) sets
is_temp=True, routing codegen through generate_operation_code() which
casts both operands to a common_type via result_as(). For C++ types
like unique_ptr, this generates ((std::unique_ptr<T>)NULL) which is
ambiguous with GCC 14 because NULL matches both the pointer and
nullptr_t constructors.

Since analyse_cpp_comparison() already coerces operands to the resolved
operator's argument types, the subsequent result_as(common_type) cast
is redundant for C++ comparisons. Skip it and use the operands'
result() directly.

Closes #7765

milestone.description:
Main features:
- Pattern matching
- Generics / subscripted container types
- `@cython.collection_type()`
- `restrict`
- more vectorcalls
- more thread safety
- smaller modules, more shared code
- remove Py3.8 support, keep Py3.9+

title: Fix ambiguous unique_ptr(NULL) comparison in C++ mode
